reasoning: The baseline for the 30-day average of riots over the trailing 360 days in Senegal is ~0, making the reference value 1.000. To resolve positive, there must be at least 11 riots in the 30 days preceding each resolution date. Currently (as of Nov 23, 2025), despite an emergent political rift between President Faye and PM Sonko regarding coalition control, there are no reports of violent street protests or riots. In fact, leaders are actively trying to de-escalate the tension. Because there are no ongoing riots, the chance of hitting 11+ riots by Nov 30 is effectively the minimum allowed probability (0.05). For the December and February dates, the probabilities are slightly higher (0.06 and 0.09) to account for the longer time horizon and the possibility of the political rift worsening into street violence, but hitting 11 riots in a single month would require a massive crisis similar to the 2023 protests, which seems unlikely given current de-escalation efforts.
